rationale: The citing paper uses Goettler and Clay (2011) as a specific methodological reference point when discussing how to model tariff choice with switching costs. In the model setup, it explicitly contrasts its own one-period expected-utility choice assumption with the forward-looking dynamic alternative in Goettler and Clay, and discusses the implication of that modeling choice for estimated switching costs. This is not a generic literature-list mention; it plays a concrete role in justifying and positioning the paper's identification and structural assumptions.
relation_description: The citing paper methodologically positions itself against Goettler and Clay's forward-looking dynamic switching-cost framework, adopting a simpler static choice horizon while acknowledging the trade-off. The cited work functions as a substantive benchmark for the alternative modeling strategy the authors choose not to implement.
